briefdescription.para: Object this handle points at.
This constructor is used by Dali New() methods.
This constructor provides an uninitialized Dali::BaseHandle.

detaileddescription.para:
This should be initialized with a Dali New() method before use. Methods called on an uninitialized Dali::BaseHandle will assert. BaseHandlehandle;//uninitialized handle.SomeMethod();//unsafe!Thiswillassert
handle=SomeClass::New();//nowinitialized handle.SomeMethod();//safe @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Dali::BaseHandle is intended as a base class.

detaileddescription.para: This is non-virtual since derived BaseHandle types must not contain data. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: This copy constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.
This assignment operator is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

detaileddescription.para:
It makes this handle use the same BaseObject as the copied handle @SINCE_1_0.0
rhs
A reference to the copied handle
A reference to this handle

briefdescription.para: Move constructor.
Move assignment operator.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_9.22
rhs
A reference to the moved handle
A reference to this handle

briefdescription.para: Connects a void() functor to a specified signal.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
connectionTracker
A connection tracker which can be used to disconnect
signalName
Name of the signal to connect to
functor
The functor to move
True if the signal was available
The signal must be available in this object.

briefdescription.para: Performs an action on this object with the given action name and attributes.

detaileddescription.para:
Usage example: - BaseHandlehandle=SomeClass::New();//InitializedwithNew()method
Property::Mapattributes;//TypeisPropertyMap handle.DoAction("show",attributes); @SINCE_1_0.0
actionName
The command for the action
attributes
The list of attributes for the action
The action is performed by the object or not

briefdescription.para: Returns the type name for the Handle.

detaileddescription.para: Will return an empty string if the typename does not exist. This will happen for types that have not registered with type-registry.

@SINCE_1_0.0
The type name. Empty string if the typename does not exist

briefdescription.para: Returns the type info for the Handle.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
info
The type information
true if the type info exists

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the internal Dali resource.

detaileddescription.para:
This is useful for checking the reference count of the internal resource. This method will not check the validity of the handle so the caller is expected to check it by using if (handle). @SINCE_1_0.0
The BaseObject which is referenced by the BaseHandle

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the internal Dali resource.

detaileddescription.para:
This is useful for checking the reference count of the internal resource. This method will not check the validity of the handle so the caller is expected to check it by using if (handle). @SINCE_1_0.0
The BaseObject which is referenced by the BaseHandle

briefdescription.para: Resets the handle.

detaileddescription.para: If no other handle copies exist, the internal Dali resource will be deleted. Calling this is not required i.e. it will happen automatically when a Dali::BaseHandle is destroyed. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Converts an handle to a bool.

detaileddescription.para: This is useful for checking whether the handle is empty. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Equality operator overload.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
rhs
A reference to the compared handle
True if the handle handles point to the same Dali resource, or if both are NULL

briefdescription.para: Inequality operator overload.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
rhs
A reference to the compared handle
True if the handle handles point to the different Dali resources

briefdescription.para: Gets the reference counted object pointer.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
A pointer to the reference counted object

briefdescription.para: Not intended for application developers.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
connectionTracker
A connection tracker which can be used to disconnect
signalName
Name of the signal to connect to
functorDelegate
A newly allocated functor delegate (takes ownership)
True if the signal was available

briefdescription.para: Dali::BaseHandle is a handle to an internal Dali resource.

detaileddescription.para: Each Dali handle consists of a single private pointer, and a set of non-virtual forwarding functions. This hides the internal implementation, so it may be modified without affecting the public interface.
Dali handles have implicit smart-pointer semantics. This avoids the need to match resource allocation methods like new/delete (the RAII idiom).
Dali handles can be copied by value. When a Dali handle is copied, both the copy and original will point to the same Dali resource.

The internal Dali resources are reference counted. copying a Dali handle will increase the reference count. A resource will not be deleted until all its Dali::BaseHandle handles are destroyed, or reset.
@SINCE_1_0.0
